fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Minnesota CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Minnesota - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer CNA license to minnesota, minnesota CNA endorsement application

board.name: Minnesota Dept of Health
board.mailing_address: Minnesota Department of Health, Nursing Assistant Registry, PO Box 64900, St. Paul, MN 55164-0900

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Minnesota.

steps.title: Complete Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Minnesota Department of Health (MDH) approved Nursing Assistant training program.

steps.title: Create Headmaster D&S Account
steps.description: Create a new user account on the Headmaster D&S website (hdmaster.com/testing/cnatesting/minnesota) to manage your exam application.

steps.title: Apply and Schedule Exam
steps.description: Apply for the Nursing Assistant Competency Evaluation Exam (NACE), which includes both a written/oral and a skills portion, and schedule your exam dates through your Headmaster D&S account. Pay the required exam fee at the time of scheduling.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Exam
steps.description: Successfully pass both the written/oral and skills portions of the Nursing Assistant Competency Evaluation Exam.

steps.title: Registry Listing
steps.description: Upon successful completion of the exam, your name will be automatically added to the Minnesota Nursing Assistant Registry by the Minnesota Department of Health (MDH).

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Minnesota CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in Minnesota. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Minnesota.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a NA in MN.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Minnesota.

hero.title: Become a NA in Minnesota
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) in Minnesota, you must complete an MDH-approved training program and register for the competency evaluation exam through the Headmaster D&S online system.

seo.title: How to Become a NA in Minnesota
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Minnesota NA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Create a new user account on the Headmaster D&S website to register for and schedule the Nursing Assistant Competency Evaluation Exam.
